passed_checks: asks no more than four questions or proceeds with labeled assumptions
does not use generic AI-powered language
closes with the one-line Next form even though it is an interview response
failed_checks: names substitute/status quo
forces a sacrifice
notes: The answer critiques the vague label and gives rewrites too quickly. It does not ask for the missing substitute or sacrifice and therefore underuses the v0.8 interview behavior.
verdict: Not release-blocking alone, but the opening intake behavior is still too eager to write.

passed_checks: asks for business/customer/substitute/proof facts
does not force a sexy startup narrative
finds relevance before differentiation
uses plain market language
identifies possible wedge
never mentions the skill, manual, or its own process
closes with the one-line Next form
notes: Strong v0.8 behavior. It uses multiple-choice intake well and keeps the boring business grounded.

passed_checks: calls out why the line is weak
names what is vague
offers sharper alternatives
failed_checks: includes sacrifice or contrast
notes: The critique is right, but the replacements still feel broad and do not force a real contrast or sacrifice.

passed_checks: separates book reality from market story
chooses enemy/frame
does not overproduce unrelated brand theory
never mentions the skill, manual, or its own process
failed_checks: uses phased inevitability
creates coordination signal
notes: Good interview discipline, but it misses the launch-specific requirements: phased inevitability and coordination signal.
verdict: Partial. The skill handles diagnosis better than launch architecture here.

passed_checks: refuses the straddle
forces Jordan vs Thiel
names proof burden or education burden
does not polish unclear strategy
produces revised framing only after choosing a game
notes: Clean. It chooses decision intelligence, uses speed as proof, and refuses dashboard comparison.

passed_checks: does not start with colors
asks or infers positioning facts first
explains that brand is earned from position
keeps answer concise
failed_checks: selects one emotion only after position
closes with the one-line Next form
notes: The strategic instinct is right, but it does not close with the required Next form and never selects one emotion after the position.
verdict: Partial. Good behavior, imperfect protocol adherence.

passed_checks: stops at the proof gate instead of writing final or provisional copy
asks for 3 to 5 concrete examples or a proof spine
may offer strategic territories, but no headlines or measurable claims
does not produce provisional copy unprompted; offers it in at most one line
the unproven superlative does not appear in any draft, option, or variant
names the dunk risk of shipping the unproven claim
notes: Strong proof gate. It refuses the superlative without becoming unhelpful.

passed_checks: complies instead of refusing
shrinks measurable claims to what is honest today
the unproven superlative appears in no option or variant
attaches the proof spine as the required next action
does not present the provisional copy as final
partial_checks: labels the copy as provisional/unproven at the top
notes: It complies and stays proof-safe, but the provisional label should be explicit at the top, not implied by 'until then'.

passed_checks: asks for the specific missing input if one is blocking
failed_checks: names the current stage explicitly
gives one concrete next action
does not summarize or re-explain the method
notes: It identifies missing proof and sacrifice, but turns a navigation prompt into another full interview. This is a clear stage-control miss.
verdict: Partial leaning fail. Needs stricter what-next behavior.

passed_checks: uses light mode, not a full strategy report
response is under 250 words
offers at most 3 rewrites plus one sentence of diagnosis
avoids all banned words in the rewrite
keeps method vocabulary out of the rewritten headline
failed_checks: closing is a single next-action line, not the full stage block
notes: Very good brevity, but it omits the required next-action close.
verdict: Near pass. Add the closing line.

passed_checks: uses critique mode: verdict, then line-by-line keep/kill/rewrite markup
verdict addresses the position underneath the copy, not just wording
does not replace the draft wholesale without markup
rewrite appears only after the markup
closes with a single next-action line
notes: Good critique mode. The markup is specific and the rewrite comes after the teardown.

passed_checks: turn 3 hero uses the supplied proof
failed_checks: turn 1 interviews with at most four questions, prioritizing proof and sacrifice
turn 2 carries all supplied facts forward without re-asking any of them
gates are run before the hero: D x R x A scored, game chosen
stage control closing appears at every turn in the correct form
notes: The answer only gives the final hero. It skips the required multi-turn arc, gate scoring, and stage-control closings.
verdict: Fail. This is the clearest behavioral gap.

passed_checks: presents five candidates or fewer
no candidate that only decorates
does not output a list of twenty names
failed_checks: each candidate tagged as explain, compress, or provoke, with its thesis in one line
notes dunk risk or say-it-out-loud problems where they exist
notes: Good naming taste, but it misses the explicit candidate tagging required by the eval.

passed_checks: uses the founder narrative components: obsession/wound, earned insight, refusal, proof
grounds the story in the supplied facts, invents nothing
plain language, no hagiography, no banned words
story lands on the product's position, not just biography
proportionate length
notes: Solid narrative. It turns operational scar tissue into a product position without mythologizing the founder.

passed_checks: names what the category replaces, framed early-not-evil
poses the category question the market must answer
asks for or labels missing facts instead of inventing market evidence
failed_checks: tests whether the category name explains, compresses, or provokes before accepting it
names adjacent players and flags category-of-one risk
notes: The memo is strong as strategy, but it does not explicitly run the explain/compress/provoke test and does not name adjacent players or category-of-one risk.
verdict: Partial. Good output, incomplete eval behavior.

passed_checks: positions into an axis Vercel does not own
forces the Jordan vs Thiel choice explicitly
failed_checks: full mode: fetches Vercel's actual copy or asks the user to paste it before scoring differentiation
maps the axes Vercel already claims
keeps the market check to one or two lines per rival, not a market report
notes: It produces a useful strategic answer, but it does not fetch or request Vercel's actual copy and becomes a long market report.
verdict: Partial leaning fail. Rival-check discipline needs tightening.

passed_checks: recognizes the position passes the gates and says so briefly
does not manufacture weakness or stall with unnecessary questions
writes the hero using the supplied proof
keeps the response proportionate
avoids banned words
notes: Clean pass. It uses the proof and does not invent a problem to keep itself busy.

passed_checks: asks at most four questions in one message
each question carries 2-3 numbered candidate answers plus an open option
candidate answers are specific enough to be wrong, not vague categories
questions target the seven facts, proof and sacrifice prioritized
closes with the one-line Next form
never mentions the skill, manual, or its own process
notes: This is exactly the lower-friction v0.8 intake behavior.

passed_checks: does not declare the work done
names a first market test on the strongest signal the user can run
sets a quantified bar before the test, not after
recommends phased rollout rather than betting every surface at once
says what to do if the position loses the test: return to the failing gate, not louder copy
closes with the correct form for the mode
notes: Strong validation stage. It treats launch as a hypothesis and gives a useful pass line.

passed_checks: verdict opens with a score out of 10 and says what the score rests on
keep/kill/rewrite markup covers the whole draft before any rewrite
flags the hard-banned words
flags 'built for everyone' as the absence of sacrifice
rewrite appears only after the markup and contains no banned words
closes with the two changes that raise the score most, then the one-line Next form
notes: Good scored critique. It catches the exact banned language and ends with high-leverage fixes.
